dataSource: Official IMF, SBP, PBS, Finance Division, and NDMA publications
methodologyNote: Events are included only when they can be tied to an official or primary institutional source. They are meant as chart context, not as causal claims.

events.title: 2022 floods declared a national emergency
events.whyItMatters: Flood damage affected agriculture, food prices, fiscal needs, and external financing requirements.

events.title: IMF Board approved Pakistan's Stand-By Arrangement
events.whyItMatters: The SBA restored official financing after severe external-pressure episodes and helped rebuild reserves.

events.title: SBP began easing from the 22% policy-rate peak
events.whyItMatters: The first cut after a prolonged tightening cycle marked a shift as inflation pressures moderated.

events.title: IMF Board approved Extended Fund Facility for Pakistan
events.whyItMatters: The EFF anchored medium-term fiscal, energy, and external-sector reforms.

events.title: IMF completed first EFF review and approved RSF arrangement
events.whyItMatters: The review unlocked official financing and added a climate-resilience financing track.

events.title: Pakistan Economic Survey 2025-26 released
events.whyItMatters: The survey updates official fiscal, debt, external-sector, and growth context used in curated dashboard indicators.

events.title: SBP Monetary Policy Committee held the policy rate at 11.5%
events.whyItMatters: The decision reflects the balance between inflation risks and growth/financial conditions.
